candidates.stmt:
Steve Campion has proudly lived in the Gateshead area for 25 years. A local business owner based in Gateshead, and understands the needs of residents and the local economy. He raised his family here, with children educated in Blaydon. He is deeply
committed to the community.

Gillian Jackson is a dedicated resident, committed to protecting local interests and ensuring community voices are heard. With a background in customer service, she understands the challenges of rising costs and public service pressures, She represents a fresh and honest approach to politics.

After years of tackling tough IT challenges, resolving them, and delivering results on tight budgets, Pedro Santos wants to bring that same drive, problem-solving energy, and commitment to our community, standing as a Reform UK candidate for councillor in Blaydon.

Rachel Cabral is our candidate for the Gateshead Central and Whickham constituency. Rachel has lived in central Gateshead for 37 years, choosing to make it her home for all of her adult life through working, raising her family and setting up a local business. She loves Gateshead, the diversity and social mix of its urban area coupled with its history, its open and rural spaces and its creative and artistic landmarks. Professionally Rachel had a thirty two year career in the Civil Service giving her valuable insight into how Government works and how it can best service citizens' needs. She took early retirement to set up and run a cafe and empathises with other small business owners rebuilding post-pandemic and dealing with the cost of living crisis. Rachel is very visible in the Gateshead community. She was a school Governor and Chair at a Gateshead school for many years, co-coordinated a network for new parents, runs writing and reading groups for users with mental health issues and has even run singing competitions in the local pub. Gateshead has some of the highest levels of poverty in the UK and Rachel works hard in the community as the lead Green campaigner, advocating for the disenfranchised. She is also a writer of prose and poetry. Having been politically active since her school days Rachel joined the Green Party nearly ten years ago, attracted by its bold and long term environmental policies and because she identified the Green Party, more than any other, as the party for fairness and social justice for everyone in our communities.

I was born in the North East, in County Durham, where I spent most of my life until moving to Gateshead. **I work exceptionally hard for my community and put my full effort into everything that I do.** Nationally I am the Co-Chair of the LGBTIQA+ Greens. Where I, alongside my other Co-Chair, **we work tirelessly to keep hard-fought rights for LGBT people.**

I’ll be continuing to listen to residents after being elected and **using my voice and position on the council to push for change that residents want to see** in the Bridges ward. It’s my objective to ensure that Gateshead is served by representatives that actually represent the people who elect them. **I won’t let you down.**

Vicky Anderson is the Liberal Democrat candidate for Blaydon and Consett. She was elected as a local Liberal Democrat Councillor in Gateshead for Dunston Hill and Whickham East in May 2019, winning against Labour. 

Local to the North East, Vicky is passionate about the region where she grew up and the wealth of talent and ambition it has to offer. Vicky also believes strongly in creating a fair, open and accepting society for all, and is a passionate advocate and campaigner for equality, LGBTQ+ and women’s rights.

Having lived in Gateshead for most of my life, I am standing in this election because I believe I can be the strong voice for Gateshead that we need in London:
- speaking up to oppose a damaging hard Brexit that will hurt people in places like Gateshead, 
- fighting for a fair deal to protect our NHS and care services, and protect those who depend on them.
- making sure our schools are properly funded, not facing budget cuts as they are at present, and able to concentrate on teaching and education for all, 
- safeguarding the environment and getting more jobs in Gateshead
- reporting back to, and keeping in touch with people in Gateshead, not taking them for granted in the way that labour has done for far too long.

I am a local resident who believes High Fell deserves a champion who truly listens. I’m standing for Council because our streets, from Sheriff Hill to Beacon Lough, feel overlooked. Whether it’s fixing broken pavements, improving our local parks, or tackling the "heat or eat" crisis, I will be a persistent voice for our community.
The Green Party offers the only real plan to provide warm, energy-efficient homes and fairer services that don’t cost the earth.

High Fell is my home; its resilience inspires me every day. Together, we can build a cleaner, safer, and more affordable future for every neighbour

Biography:

I am a plumber and heating engineer working in many areas of the north east of England. 

Up until 2016 I was apolitical, with no strong political views and certainly none that I wanted to be vocal about. 

Then came 2016. 

Having carefully listened to both sides of the Brexit debate, I decided it would be a good idea for Britain to leave the EU. I therefore voted to leave. Having done so, when the results were announced, I was happy that the UK had signalled its intention to leave the European Union. 

To let you into a secret, at that time I would have accepted the result even if we had lost. 

However, we won. 

I then watched 3 years of lies and deception as politicians and civil servants went out of their way to stall the process. I could see what was happening and felt powerless and increasingly annoyed. Then came Boris with his oven ready Brexit deal which again was a farce but got him into power. Again, I thought, well that’s the best we are going to get. Then came Covid. As a plumber, I worked all the way through and took only the first tranche of the relief payments.

The government asked whether my business had been adversely affected; as it was not, I pursued no further payments. I then watched the government give millions away to con men and Conservative Party members and then make up lies to keep us controlled. All this time I WAS getting increasingly frustrated with the self-serving political class. 

Meanwhile general elections came and went, local elections came and went. There was never an option to vote for Reform UK. I thought, that if I didn’t feel I could ever vote Conservative or Labour and there wasn’t a candidate for Reform UK there must many more people that felt the same as me. I therefore put myself forward as a Reform Party candidate, not for myself but to give all those unhappy with the two major parties someone, and indeed something, to vote for. 

Throughout this period there have been immigrants arriving every day and ag

𝙈𝙚𝙚𝙩 𝙋𝙚𝙩𝙚𝙧 𝙍𝙞𝙙𝙙𝙚𝙣 - I have lived in “Old Wardley” and Leam Lane for over 40 years, and like many of you, this isn’t just where I live it’s home. My family and friends are here, and I’ve grown up between both communities, giving me a real understanding of the people and the issues that matter most.

I am standing as a Labour candidate because I’ve always listened to my neighbours and the wider community, and I want to do more to support and fight for our area.

I may be new to standing for election, but I am not new to taking action locally. I’ve helped campaign to save the 69 bus service, which so many residents rely on. I also successfully pushed for a zebra crossing outside the QE Hospital after seeing how difficult it was for people to cross safely.

I’ve supported residents with individual issues too from raising housing concerns with the council to advocating for someone needing additional support. I’ve also worked with local people to raise concerns about housing developments and ensure residents’ voices are heard.

For me, one of the biggest issues facing our community is 𝗮𝗻𝘁𝗶 𝘀𝗼𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗹 𝗯𝗲𝗵𝗮𝘃𝗶𝗼𝘂𝗿. We need to feel safe where we live. I want to work closely with local police and residents to take a firm stand against this and make our area safer for everyone. Alongside this, I want to tackle 𝗹𝗶𝘁𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗶𝗻𝗴, improve our 𝘀𝘁𝗿𝗲𝗲𝘁𝘀, and increase 𝘆𝗼𝘂𝘁𝗵 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝘃𝗶𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻 so young people have better opportunities and support.

I work in healthcare as a Biomedical Scientist, with a background in public service, and I care deeply about helping others. I’ve also been involved in local activities like theatre at Heworth Grange Comprehensive School and singing with Northern Proud Voices, and I’m passionate about encouraging young people including speaking at local events to support those interested in careers in science.

I’m proud to be standing alongside Councillor Jill Green and Councillor Sharron Potts as part of a strong local Labour team. Together, we can continue working hard for Wa
